discussion_comments.comment_body: i added this to track pending codebase index statuses because i was seeing issues where we're still initializing and losing the snapshot
As mentioned above, we should fix the root cause of this. Transitioning from initializing to connected should be synchronous once we receive the initialization response
ok i tried to fix this, see `crates/remote_server/src/manager.rs` line 198. lmk if you think that isn't the right approach
we synced, going to move `event_rx` to be returned from `run_connect_and_handshake`

Looks good overall, nice find! I wonder if there's a little potential for trouble in the fact that we break updates into discovered and deleted rules? This loses the ordering of those updates so an (add, delete) of the same rule ends up looking just like a (delete, add) in the emitted event. Might be better before adding to either list to dedupe?

If we were already in agent view for this conversation, the controller short-circuits without emitting `EnteredAgentView`, so the`BlocklistAIInputModel` subscription that normally locks the input on entry never fires. Therefore, lock the input explicitly here instead of in [input_models.rs ](https://github.com/warpdotdev/warp/pull/9865/changes/6f4f7db3be130748fc4a61a9eab10722657167cf#diff-3b7a5ecea2927912fa8b51369e02d78432a998fb0746f83ca464c4904b480812)
these arefs calls that shouldn't happen on the main thread
does `is_absolute()` require a syscall? if so we should refactor this to first collect paths from the conversation, then do validation (or any syscall-requiring work) async
no it's just a string check (based on platform)
no it's just a string check (based on platform)
